body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
irrlicht_observes: yes

## Primitive

`multi_model_orchestration` (per-turn model selection): the agent can change models between turns and the transcript reflects the new model in each turn's metadata.

## agent_supports â€” yes

codex 0.130.0 ships an interactive `/model` slash command in the TUI. Verified live (tmux): booting `codex --no-alt-screen` shows `model: gpt-5.5 high   /model to change`. Sending `/model` opens a two-step picker â€” first a model list (gpt-5.5 current, gpt-5.4, gpt-5.4-mini, gpt-5.3-codex, gpt-5.2), then a reasoning-level list (Low / Medium / High / Extra high). Selecting gpt-5.3-codex + Medium prints `â€˘ Model changed to gpt-5.3-codex medium` and the input footer flips to `gpt-5.3-codex medium`. A subsequent turn runs on the new model. There is also a launch-time `-m/--model` flag and `-c model=<name>` config override, but mid-session switching is the canonical primitive and it works.

## irrlicht_observes â€” yes

The codex rollout writes the active model into a per-turn `turn_context` event at `payload.model` (verified in a live rollout: `{"type":"turn_context","payload":{...,"model":"gpt-5.3-codex",...}}`). NOTE: in this codex version the model is NOT on `token_count`/usage events (`payload.info` has no model key) â€” it is carried solely by `turn_context` and the `session_meta` header carries only `model_provider`, not the model name.

The daemon still observes it: `extractCodexMetadata` (parser.go:263) reads `payload.model` for any payload-wrapped event, so it sets `ev.ModelName` on the `turn_context` line. The parser marks `turn_context` Skip=true (parser.go:174), but the tailer's Skip branch deliberately still calls `applyMetadata` â†’ `applyModelMetadata` (tailer.go:467-478, tailer_metrics.go:104), which sets `t.metrics.ModelName = NormalizeModelName(parsed.ModelName)`. So each turn's `turn_context` updates the session's ModelName, and a mid-session switch surfaces as a model_name change. The replay golden captures this at `summary.model_name` (confirmed `gpt-5.5` in the existing multi-turn-conversation golden), which is what `replay-fixtures.sh` diffs.

## Observability caveat â€” where the assertion lives

The `expected.jsonl` validator DSL (validate/expected.go Phase struct: phase / expected_state / kind / max_delay_ms / duration_at_least_ms / same_session_as / new_session / invariants) operates over the lifecycle `events.jsonl` stream, which carries NO model_name field â€” so expected.jsonl cannot directly assert `model A â†’ model B`. The model-switch proof is the metrics-level `summary.model_name` in the `.replay.json.golden` snapshot (final value = model B), validated by replay-fixtures. expected.jsonl therefore asserts the lifecycle skeleton (turn 1 workingâ†’ready, /model picker, turn 2 workingâ†’ready, same session) and the model transition is the golden's job.

## Conclusion

applicable: yes. multi_model_orchestration = yes.
caveats: In codex 0.130.0 the model name lives only on per-turn turn_context events (Skip=true in the parser); token_count/usage events carry no model. Observability relies on the tailer applying ModelName from Skip'd events â€” correct today, but a refactor that drops metadata on skipped events would silently regress this.

/model is an interactive two-step picker (model list, then reasoning-level list), not a one-shot `/model <name>` command like aider. The codex driver's send/slash steps type a literal line + Enter, so the picker must be driven with explicit number-key + Enter send steps.

sources.note: Skip branch still calls applyMetadata for skipped events (L467-478)
Phase struct has no model field â€” DSL asserts state/kind on events.jsonl, not model_name
golden carries summary.model_name (gpt-5.5) â€” the metrics-level model artifact replay-fixtures diffs
/model picker switched gpt-5.5â†’gpt-5.3-codex; 'â€˘ Model changed to gpt-5.3-codex medium'; rollout turn_context.payload.model='gpt-5.3-codex'
